title: cornus stolonifera f. californica
intro: Cornus stolonifera f. californica, also known as the red osier dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cornus stolonifera f. californica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve its bark being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
